projects.title: Webshop.
projects.description:
Making this fully clickable webshop prototype in Figma helped me understand how a real website works, not just how it looks. I connected all the pages so users can click through categories, view products, and move between screens easily.

This project also showed me how small design details like buttons, hover effects, and transitions can make a big difference. It helped me focus on creating a simple, clean, and user-friendly experience.

projects.title: Photoshop Posters
projects.description:
For this project, I wanted to improve my skills in Adobe Photoshop while experimenting more with graphic design. I didn’t have much experience with Photoshop yet, so this was a good way to learn by doing.

By creating three different posters, I explored different styles like photo-based design, minimalism, and collage. I worked with layers, masking, typography, textures, and composition, which helped me better understand both Photoshop and design basics.

projects.title: Samen aan Z
projects.description:
During my internship, I worked on 'Samen aan Z', a data collection platform for researchers to gather insights from hospitals to improve staff well-being and reduce burnout.

In 5 weeks, I worked on researching and redesigning the platform, and then helped build it using Angular (Module Federation) and Tailwind CSS. The project was a collaboration with Fontys Research Groups and the company Stormin, funded by the government.

Through this project, I learned a lot about working with frameworks, setting up scalable foundations, and building a system that can grow into a larger platform that not only uses surveys, but also other data sources in the future.

projects.title: Sprout Up
projects.description:
Sprout Up is an app concept focused on promoting plant growth in a fun and engaging way. Users can collect trees in the app and earn in-game money, which they can use to unlock and grow more trees.

The idea behind the app is that when a user buys a tree, a real tree is planted in that country. This creates a sense of impact, where users are not only collecting digitally but also contributing to real-world environmental growth.

Through this project, I worked on designing the app in Figma and building it using Flutter. It helped me understand how to turn a concept into an interactive app while focusing on both user engagement and meaningful impact.

projects.title: Blue Lock Cards
projects.description:
This was an early passion project where I designed and built a website for collecting exclusive Blue Lock cards. Users can slide through different cards and explore characters in an interactive way.

I went through multiple design iterations, improving the layout and interactions based on feedback from others. This helped me understand how small changes can improve the overall user experience.

I built the final version using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to test my understanding of the basics. Since this was before AI tools were common, it really pushed me to figure things out myself and strengthen my foundation in front-end development.
